reasons: Angela can attach to Benedetta during a dive, giving extra protection while Benedetta enters and exits fights.
Benedetta's mobility makes Angela's attached pressure harder for enemies to isolate.

proof.summary: Benedetta dives or chases with mobility, while Angela adds a shield and support layer during the high-risk entry window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Benedetta commits deeply.
Benedetta uses mobility to stay active instead of disengaging too early.
failureCases: Benedetta is burst before Angela finishes channeling.
The enemy waits out Angela's shield before re-engaging.

reasons: Floryn can support Benedetta during side-lane or dive fights without needing to stand beside her.
Benedetta benefits from extra healing because her value often depends on surviving long enough to reset position.

proof.summary: Benedetta creates mobile pressure, while Floryn provides healing support that helps her survive extended skirmishes.
worksBestWhen: Floryn saves healing for Benedetta's commit or escape window.
Benedetta takes fights near meaningful objectives or side-lane pressure.
failureCases: Benedetta dives without a retreat path.
Floryn uses heal before Benedetta is actually pressured.

reasons: Mathilda adds another movement and shield layer to Benedetta's mobile fight pattern.
This synergy helps Benedetta enter, reposition, or disengage after forcing enemy skills.

proof.summary: Benedetta uses her own dashes to pressure, while Mathilda gives the team extra shield and movement options around the dive.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda times Guiding Wind around Benedetta's commit.
Benedetta uses the movement option to escape after forcing cooldowns.
failureCases: Both heroes spend mobility tools at the same time with no follow-up.
The enemy chains hard CC after the dash windows end.

reasons: Benedetta can commit aggressively when Faramis gives the team a safety layer during grouped fights.
Faramis helps reduce the risk of Benedetta being punished after she dives into the enemy formation.

proof.summary: Benedetta pressures the enemy backline, while Faramis gives teamfight insurance if the dive turns into a full commit.
worksBestWhen: Benedetta fights near Faramis's teamfight area.
Faramis casts protection before the team collapses.
failureCases: Benedetta fights too far from the team.
Faramis is picked off before the fight.
The team disengages instead of using the protection window.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies, giving Benedetta a clear path for Alecto: Final Blow.
Benedetta can follow Atlas's engage from an angle instead of starting the fight alone.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies with AoE CC, while Benedetta follows through the controlled area with dash damage and zone pressure.
worksBestWhen: Benedetta waits for Atlas to commit first.
Atlas catches targets along a path Benedetta can dash through.
failureCases: Benedetta enters before Atlas lands CC.
Atlas pulls enemies away from Benedetta's angle.
The enemy saves mobility after Atlas's pull.
